Okay, now we're going to cover ... this is just going to be a deep dive into how to promote
other people's launches as a Joint Venture partner or as an affiliate. I'm going to walk
through the formula we use to just completely crush it when we promote for
other people's launches. We just do really, really well, but also because of our launches, for
Product Launch Formula® and their other products, we get to see what works when other
people promote for us. We get to see all this data because we're in the middle of it. We get to see
the traffic coming in. We get to see the sales coming in. We get to watch what their strategies are
and what their bonuses are, and how they're doing it and how the sales flow and how they come
and go. Basically, this is based both on our experience as affiliates and as partners but also as
product creators and we get to see both sides of the fence. You're going to get the benefit. I'm
going to walk through how we get the results and how we see other people getting results.
Another thing here is I'm going to be using Product Launch Formula as an example as we
go through this because you are invited to be one of our affiliates which is a pretty big deal.
Normally, we have a closed system. People, not everyone can be an affiliate for us. "Closed"
probably isn't the right word. I guess, it's just people have to generally come in on referrals. We
have a big long application process. As one of our PLF Owners, you can get into our
system. Basically, we're opening it up for you. In here, during this deep dive, I'm going to be
giving you some examples of Product Launch Formula because I'd love to see you promote and
do really, really well and have a really effective promotion.
We have a small number of affiliates. We do not have thousands of affiliates because
they're hand selected. We have a careful screening process. It's hard to get approved as an
affiliate for us. As I mentioned, as a PLF Owner, you can get in and be one of our partners. Our
launch schedule is typically, we generally launch once a year. Sometimes, we have two launches
a year. Generally, it is once a year. It can change but that's the way it is.
We also have Evergreen Launches. We're doing these in the background. That means you
can send us traffic year round and it can convert into sales year round. Even if you sent us traffic
year round, you probably want to jump on board when we do do our big launches because the
conversions in those launches tend to be breathtaking and we have really big affiliate prizes and
we have a big leaderboard. If you can show up on our leaderboard, it will give you
instant positioning within the industry.

Another big thing is we track for other offers as well. If someone doesn't ... if you

got, they don't expire for like 999 days or something like that.
Product Launch Formula converts really, really well and it makes people

Let's get on with the deep dive. First, I want to talk about when you're going to promote
during a launch via email or social media. I'll also talk about using paid traffic and direct
referrals in a little bit, but first we're going to talk about how to promote when you're promoting
via social or via email. Of course, email is my absolute favorite. Social can work. It works for
some people better than others, but email is the absolute king. If you look at who's winning
the big launches and hitting the top of the leader boards, it's email.
Now, that being said, if you don't have a big list, that's fine. You can still really do well

make four sales and that's pretty awesome.

PROMOTING OTHER PEOPLE’S LAUNCHES
Deepdive
Anyways, first we're going to start off with email and social media. What you want to do
is really simple. You send people into each piece of Prelaunch Content, each video. For Product
Launch Formula, we have three videos. It's the classic PLF Launch. We have three videos. Be
sure to send people into each piece of Prelaunch Content. In fact, you might want to send two
emails into each Prelaunch Content. When we do a launch, typically we'll release that first piece
of Prelaunch Content and then there might be about four days or even five days until the second
piece. You can send people into that first piece of Prelaunch Content on the day that it goes live,
and then a couple of days later, send another email that says, "Hey, you might have missed this.
Did you see this?" Send another email about it.
Another thing you can do, depending on your email system, you could send to your un-
open’s. Say you're in AWeber or many other systems, you can actually segment out the people
that didn't open your email and just send another email to them with a different subject line.
They didn't open that first one so the subject line didn't move them, so come up with a different
subject line that comes at it from a different angle. You can mostly send the same copy. You do
want to change the copy up a little bit. At least change one word in each paragraph. I don't want
to get into the technical piece of that but that can help you with your Gmail and how well it does
in Gmail if you change at least one word in each paragraph, and put in a new subject line for the
un-open’s.
You can also send to the non-clicks. You can segment out the people that didn't click. In
this case, you probably want a different subject line and different copy that comes at it from a
different angle. Often, just sending to un-open’s or non-clicks can really help your performance.
Maybe for the first video you send, and then a couple days later you send to the un-open’s, and
then do the same thing for the second video and the same thing for the third video.
When you're emailing about Product Launch Formula or about any other
launch you do, make it personal. Talk about your personal experience. Talk about why you
think it's a great product. If you can tell a story about the product or about the
product creator or your interaction with it, that makes it a lot more powerful and
your list is going to resonate with it a lot more. Do you have experience with the
product? Did you take the product? Did you use PLF? Even if you didn't do a full-out launch, did
you use it to start your list-building? Did you use it to get more engagement with your people on
your email list, with your visitors, with your blog visitors? Did you use it to enhance the level of
traction, the level of interaction, the level of traffic that you're getting?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

Do you have experience with the company? Maybe you don't have direct experience with
the product, but you've got experience with the company. If you're promoting for Product
Launch Formula, maybe you've had some interactions with me via social or via email, or live at
an event. Maybe you saw me speak. What kind of experience … how can you make it personal?
Why do you think the product is important? Especially, why is it important to
your followers? To your readers? Why do you think it's going to move the needle for them?
Why is it going to make a difference for them? How's it going to help them out?
Did you get results from the product? This can be super, super powerful. I recently
promoted a product for my friend, Eben Pagan. I could say, boy, five years ago I went through
this product and it makes a difference. It made a huge difference. In fact, I still, to this day, 80%
of my days I use what I learned in this product. I built my entire promotion around that theme.
It's true. It's 100% true. I did this product, I learned from it, I put it in place in my life, and
almost everyday I use the stuff I've learned in that product.
It made a huge difference in my life and so, basically, I promote it for this. I was the
number one affiliate. We just absolutely crushed the promotion. I think I mailed three times in
the prelaunch for each piece of Prelaunch Content. Then I probably mailed when the cart
opened, and then I mailed a day before the Cart Close and twice the day it closed. Each one of
those emails was just me expounding on that theme. Me talking about that theme … that this
product made an unbelievable difference in my life and it continues to do so nearly everyday of
my life.
That was a lay-up. It was a set up. It was perfect. It was like, Eben came to me, he said,
"Do you want to promote this?" I mean, "Yes, yeah I want to promote it. I've got the perfect story
to tell." I for one, I'm looking to whether I want to promote our product becomes ... one of the
big things is, do I have a story to tell, either about the product, about the creator or about the
company. What story can I tell? What's my personal connection to it. When you can put together
that kind of promotion, you absolutely win.
As a PLF Owner, if you're going to promote Product Launch Formula, this is perfect for
you because you have direct connection to the product. You've gone through it. You've bought
the product, you've gone through the product. Obviously if you're still watching at this point in
the product, if you're still watching this video, you like the product and you think highly of the
product. You think highly of me. Maybe you've had great interaction with me or one of my
coaches or with my customer service or with the other PLF Owners. You have a story. You just
have to reflect on what is that story, what's that connection and how can I tie that in to my

Deepdive
result. This works for any launch you're doing, any product you're promoting but it just works ...
I'm saying it's built in for you because you've gone through PLF. If Product Launch Formula is a
fit for you, that's the way you can go about it.
Remember, you send people to each piece of Prelaunch Content, each video, maybe twice
to each video. Then, for the Open Cart, and if you're promoting Product Launch Formula that's
when we open cart for whatever the launch is that you're promoting, you definitely want to mail
at the Open Cart. When the launch goes live, you absolutely want to mail then. That's usually
when we mention our bonus offer. We'll send a short e-mail that says, "The cart is open. P.S., If
you buy through this link I've given you below, then I've got this extra bonus for you and it's
really awesome." We usually give some copy ... you can't just say, "This is a bonus", you have to
tell people why the bonus is really cool, and how it's going to impact them, how it's going to help
them, what difference it's going to make in their life. You just can't say, "Oh, by the way, we're
giving this teleseminar bonus". You've got to say, "This is an awesome, awesome product and if
you buy through my link I'm going to give you this great teleseminar that teaches you this, this,
this and this and helps you get this, this and this.” What result are people going to get
from that bonus? I’m also, part of this overall thing, I'm giving you, there's a video just on
bonus strategies.
Send people ... usually, for Product Launch Formula we keep the cart open for about five
days. That's a typical open for us. Sometimes it's shorter. I don't know if it's ever been longer,
but, for five days, the best way to promote for that is send people to the cart, to the Open Cart.
Again, in the pre-launch, you're mailing each piece of Prelaunch Content. Once the cart opens,
you mail them the Open Cart. Then, maybe on day two or day three, you would mail to
additional content.
Usually, when we do a launch, we create additional content for our partners to mail to
during the Cart Open, during the cart week. It's easy to mail at the Cart Open, and it's easy to
mail at the Cart Close, but the time in between can be a bit of a lull, can be a bit of a slow down.
That's why we create extra content just for our partners to mail. Hopefully, when you're
promoting on other launches, they do something similar for you, because it's great to keep up
that momentum during that Open Cart period.
Lately, the last couple times we've opened we've opened on a Monday, we've closed on a
Friday. Monday, people mail on Open Cart. Tuesday, we usually have some type of social proof
message. Wednesday, we usually release new content. Either on Tuesday or Thursday we might
do a webcast or a webinar. On Friday is obviously the Close Cart. Send people to additional cart

PROMOTING OTHER PEOPLE’S LAUNCHES
Deepdive
content during the Cart Open. Then, as you get closer to the close, you want to start talking
about the scarcity of the Cart Close. Of course, not all launches have the Cart Close, but all good
launches there is a definitive end to the launch. The launch offer goes away at some level.
Bonuses go away, prices go up or the cart closes.
If you're promoting PLF we close the cart so that we can actually start our class a couple
days later. If we're going to close the cart on Friday, then on Thursday you want to start mailing
about the scarcity of the cart closing. It's like, "This is a great offer", you can have your
connection story, you can talk about your bonuses, and, "P.S. Registration closes tomorrow so
this is your last chance. You have to jump on board. This is a great offer, and if you want to get
on board and change your life then you've got to act now because registration closes down
tomorrow.” Usually, when you do this you will get a little bump in sales, but in a way, you're
really setting the stage for the next day because this message gets that deadline in their mind
and the next day is when the cart closing day is when the action really starts to happen.
On the cart closing day, you need to mail two or three times. Typically, what we do when
we're promoting for someone, we mail early in the morning. We mail at maybe six or seven a.m.
Eastern U.S. time, so very, very early in the morning for the U.S. Then, we'll mail typically mid-
day, maybe one or two o'clock Eastern time. It might be six, seven, eight hours after that first e-
mail and then we'll mail in the evening of the Cart Close.
Typically, we'll mail at maybe six or seven p.m Eastern time, so another five or six hours
after that second e-mail. This will double your results. It will typically double results if ...
Compared to not mailing on the closing day or mailing just once on the closing day, you'll
double your results. It makes an unbelievable difference when you do this. It really comes down
to that first e-mail when the cart opens and then really, four e-mails at the end. One the day
before the close, and three the day of the close.
I know some of you are thinking, "Oh my gosh, I've never mailed my list more than once
in a week." That's okay. This will ... if you want to, you can mail just twice on the last day. The
last is, ”Just a courtesy reminder. This is a fantastic offer for you. I really love this product. It
made a huge difference for me. I love it so much I'm standing behind it. I put together a bonus
package for you. Here's the bonus that you get. I just want to give you this final courtesy
reminder that is registration is closing tonight. You have to jump on board today. This the time.
You don't have any more time. By the way, if you want to get my bonus, this is the only way to
get my bonus is to jump on board right now." When you do that, you'll get huge results and your

Deepdive
people will thank you. If you're really feeling squeamish about mailing that third time, maybe
just mail to un-opens or non-clicks with your third e-mail.
Next up, you really need to offer a bonus. I put together an entire video about bonuses,
and you watch that. You don't always need to offer a bonus. Just a simple promotion can often
do it, but generally offering a bonus can really move the needle. One of the things you can
do is stack your bonus during your Cart Open period. That means adding to your
bonus as you near the end of the launch. Maybe at the Cart Open, you can say, "Buy
through my link and I'm going to give you this bonus or these two bonuses." Then, as you move
through the launch, maybe if we open on Monday, on Wednesday or Tuesday you can mail and
say, "You know what? I decided I'm adding another bonus just to sweeten the pot, make it more
awesome for you, I'm adding another bonus." Stacking bonuses can be really powerful and it can
give you additional reasons to continue to mail.
That was basically, if we go back up, that was promoting via social media or via an e-mail
list. When promoting via paid traffic, we're going to go quicker since this is basically a subset of
what we just talked about. This is, you send people to each piece of Prelaunch Content. If you're
promoting ... it just gives you additional things ... in paid traffic these days it's often done via
Facebook. It changes over the years but Facebook is the hot one right now. Put up a post, or put
up an ad for each piece of Prelaunch Content, similar to like if you had an e-mail list. Then,
definitely send people to the Open Cart. Then, in the last 48 hours you really focus, before the
cart closes, you really want to focus on the scarcity. Change your ads so they really focus on the
scarcity and the close in that final 48 hours.
It's very, very similar to promoting via e-mail. You can't mail un-opens, you can't mail
non-clicks and you can't build this momentum with your list where your list knows you're going
through this launch, you're promoting this entire launch and you have this overall narrative. It's
more each, when you're doing paid, it's each one, each ad has to stand alone because you don't
know who’s going to see that ad. You don't know exactly when they're going to see that ad. Each
ad has to stand alone and you're more dependent on the heavy lifting that you get from the
launch itself.
Bonus strategies don't really work with paid traffic, unless you use the
paid ads to take people into your site, ad them to a list and then promote. Then
you can do bonus. Otherwise, if someone is just on Facebook and they're clicking your ad and
they're going straight to the launch, straight to the Prelaunch Content, straight to the sales letter,
there's no place in there to make the offer of what your bonus is. You could conceivably try to do

Promoting via a direct referral. This could be a friend, it could be an associate, it
could be a partner, it could be anything like that. Someone that you know and you're either
talking to them or you're sending them direct e-mail or you're texting them or you're skyping
with them. That's where all this is. This really does come ... it's very, very similar to
doing it via e-mail, it's just it's the personal touch. You can send them to each piece of
Prelaunch Content, each video. However you're interacting with them, whether it's text message
or Skype or personal e-mail or talking to them on the phone, doesn't matter. Tell them about
each piece of Prelaunch Content, get them excited. Especially the first piece of Prelaunch
Content. Bring them into that first piece and get them started in the overall narrative of the
launch.
Then, tell them about the Open Cart. Certainly, warn them about that Cart Close. I don't
know if you want to text your friends two or three times, but maybe once the day before the close
and once the day of the close. "Hey, did you jump on board this. This thing, this is awesome."
You can still offer a bonus, even if it's a friend of yours or if it's a work associate. You can just
say, "You know what? I feel so strongly about this. You go ahead and do this sign up for it, and
I'm going to be your study buddy. I'm going to help you walk you through it. I'll answer your
questions." It's just the real way you do this, it's a personal referral, but you can still offer the
bonus.
Okay, next up, when promoting during Evergreen you can send people into
the opt-in page. When we're doing our Evergreen Launches, you can just send
your traffic, you can use your affiliate link and send them into our opt-in page
for our first piece of Prelaunch Content. Another thing you can do, you can do that with a
broadcast or social media, but you can also build into your autoresponder sequence. If you have
an autoresponder sequence that people get after they join your list, or after they buy your
product, just at the end of the sequence just put an e-mail in there that says, "Hey, Product
Launch Formula is awesome. I've had incredible results. I love the product. The people are
amazing. Jeff gives you great support, yada yada."
Whatever your experience is, go check or just, "If you want some really great training on
how to do a Product Launch, or how to do a Business Launch, or how to create a business online,
go here and check out there's this great free video series and these three videos are awesome",
